steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition. The previous sentence says scholars affirm the co-op model's economic sustainability while acknowledging its challenges — a balanced view. The sentence after the blank describes people who ignore one side: those declaring the model unviable discount successes; those championing it understate challenges. The blank must signal a contrast with that balanced scholar view.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'That said, some experts overlook such nuance;': 'That said' signals a complicating contrast — yes, scholars are balanced, but others are not. 'Such nuance' directly refers back to the balanced view. The semicolon connects smoothly to the examples that follow. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Nevertheless, how these challenges might be overcome remains contentious;': Pivots to an unrelated claim about overcoming challenges; the rest of the sentence is about ignoring evidence, not solving problems. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Ultimately, these complex issues require complex solutions;': Introduces a solution-focused claim that nothing in the passage supports. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Thus, the scholars are skeptical about the co-op model's viability;': Directly reverses what was just said — the scholars affirm the model's sustainability, they are not skeptical. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
